title: Algebra: Use Properties to Add
essential_question: How can you add more than two addends?

pretest.question: Which shows the Commutative Property of Addition?

expected_errors.citation: NCTM OA Progressions K-5 p.6 — 'Students may conflate the two addition properties when they only see symbolic examples without concrete/pictorial models.'
expected_errors.note: B: Associative Property를 Commutative Property로 혼동

expected_errors.citation: NCTM OA Progressions K-5 p.6 — 'Students may conflate the two addition properties when they only see symbolic examples without concrete/pictorial models.'
expected_errors.note: C: Identity Property(+0)를 Commutative Property로 혼동
D: place value 분해(15=10+5)는 덧셈 성질이 아님

solution_steps: Commutative Property: you can change the ORDER of addends
15 + 28 = 28 + 15 — the order changed, the sum stays the same
pretest.hint: Commutative means you can switch the order.

assessment_source.name: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Sample Items — 3.NBT.A.2 Properties and Multi-Addend Addition

verification.math_note: 성질 식별 문항 — 산술 검증 불필요. Commutative: a+b=b+a 구조 확인.

pretest.question: Which shows the Associative Property of Addition?

expected_errors.citation: NCTM OA Progressions K-5 p.6 — 'Students may conflate the two addition properties when they only see symbolic examples without concrete/pictorial models.'
expected_errors.note: A: Commutative Property를 Associative Property로 혼동

expected_errors.citation: NCTM OA Progressions K-5 p.6 — 'Students may conflate the two addition properties when they only see symbolic examples without concrete/pictorial models.'
expected_errors.note: C: Identity Property(+0)를 Associative Property로 혼동
D: place value 분해는 덧셈 성질이 아님

solution_steps: Associative Property: you can change the GROUPING of addends
(86+7)+93 = 86+(7+93) — the parentheses moved, sum stays the same
pretest.hint: Associative means you can regroup (move the parentheses).

learn.title: Adding More Than Two Numbers
learn.content: Sometimes you need to add three or more numbers. You can use addition properties to make this easier. Properties are rules that are always true.

visual_data.description: Three groups of objects (apples, peaches, bananas) being combined

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 1 Lesson 5 — Properties of Addition

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 5 — Applying Properties to Add Three or More Addends

assessment_source.name: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Sample Items — 3.NBT.A.2 Properties and Multi-Addend Addition

learn.title: Commutative Property (Order Property)
learn.content: The Commutative Property says you can add numbers in any order and get the same sum. Example: 4 + 7 = 7 + 4. Both equal 11. This helps because you can rearrange numbers to find easier pairs.

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 1 Lesson 5 — Properties of Addition

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 5 — Applying Properties to Add Three or More Addends

assessment_source.name: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Sample Items — 3.NBT.A.2 Properties and Multi-Addend Addition

learn.title: Associative Property (Grouping Property)
learn.content: The Associative Property says you can group addends differently and get the same sum. Example: (5 + 3) + 7 = 5 + (3 + 7). Left: 8 + 7 = 15. Right: 5 + 10 = 15. Same answer! Grouping 3 + 7 first makes a friendly 10.

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 1 Lesson 5 — Properties of Addition

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 5 — Applying Properties to Add Three or More Addends

assessment_source.name: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Sample Items — 3.NBT.A.2 Properties and Multi-Addend Addition

learn.title: Identity Property
learn.content: The Identity Property says that adding 0 to any number gives that same number. Example: 42 + 0 = 42. Zero is the 'identity' element for addition because it doesn't change the number.

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 1 Lesson 5 — Properties of Addition

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 5 — Applying Properties to Add Three or More Addends

assessment_source.name: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Sample Items — 3.NBT.A.2 Properties and Multi-Addend Addition

learn.title: Using Properties Together: Example
learn.content: Find 36 + 37 + 51. Step 1: Line up by place value. Step 2: Look for friendly groups. 37 + 51: 7 + 1 = 8 (easy ones). Step 3: Change grouping → 36 + (37 + 51) = 36 + 88 = 124. Or reorder: (36 + 51) + 37 = 87 + 37 = 124.

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 1 Lesson 5 — Properties of Addition

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 5 — Applying Properties to Add Three or More Addends

assessment_source.name: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Sample Items — 3.NBT.A.2 Properties and Multi-Addend Addition

learn.title: Strategy: Look for Tens
learn.content: When adding 3 or more numbers, look for pairs that make a ten or a hundred. Example: 18 + 39 + 32. Notice 18 + 32 = 50 (a friendly number!). Reorder: (18 + 32) + 39 = 50 + 39 = 89.

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 1 Lesson 5 — Properties of Addition

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 5 — Applying Properties to Add Three or More Addends

assessment_source.name: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Sample Items — 3.NBT.A.2 Properties and Multi-Addend Addition

learn.title: Four Addends
learn.content: You can use properties with four or more addends too! Example: 33 + 71 + 56 + 29. Look for pairs: 71 + 29 = 100! Reorder: (71 + 29) + (33 + 56) = 100 + 89 = 189.

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 1 Lesson 5 — Properties of Addition

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 5 — Applying Properties to Add Three or More Addends

assessment_source.name: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Sample Items — 3.NBT.A.2 Properties and Multi-Addend Addition

learn.math_talk_prompt: You found that 71 + 29 = 100 and grouped (33 + 56) together. How did you spot those pairs so quickly? If the four numbers were 37, 64, 63, and 26 instead, which pairs would you group, and why? Explain your strategy for finding friendly pairs in any set of numbers.

learn.title: Key Idea Summary
learn.content: Three properties help you add: Commutative (change order), Associative (change grouping), Identity (add 0). Strategy: look for pairs that make 10, 50, or 100, then reorder and regroup to add mentally.

practice_r3.question: Amy says: '(3 + 5) × 2 = 3 + (5 × 2) because of the Associative Property.' Is Amy correct?

choices.A: Yes, the Associative Property works for all operations
choices.B: No, the Associative Property only works within the same operation

expected_errors.note: A: '결합법칙은 모든 연산에 성립' — 혼합 연산에는 성립하지 않음; 계산해 보면 16≠13
C: '묶기는 상관없음' — 연산이 다를 때 묶기는 결과를 바꿈
D: '3+5=8이 아니다'는 틀린 이유; 3+5=8이 맞고, 쟁점은 혼합 연산에서 결합법칙 불성립

solution_steps: The Associative Property works for all addition OR all multiplication, not mixed operations
practice_r3.hint: Calculate both sides. Are they equal?

D: '가장 큰 수부터 시작' — 친숙한 합(100)을 만드는 쌍을 찾지 않음

solution_steps: This is the easiest mental math approach
practice_r3.hint: Which pairs of numbers add up to exactly 100?

practice_r3.question: Does the Commutative Property work for subtraction? Which example proves your answer?

choices.B: No: 8 - 3 = 5, but 3 - 8 is not possible with whole numbers
choices.D: No: but it doesn't matter because we only need it for addition

expected_errors.note: A: '8-3=3-8=5' — 3-8은 5가 아님; 뺄셈은 교환법칙 성립 안 함
D: '맞긴 한데 덧셈에서만 필요' — 성립하지 않음을 바르게 봤지만 이유가 틀림

solution_steps: Test: 3 - 8 = ? (can't do with whole numbers in 3rd grade)
So the Commutative Property does NOT work for subtraction
practice_r3.hint: Try switching the numbers in a subtraction. Do you get the same answer?

concept_source.name: Illustrative Mathematics G3 Unit 1 Lesson 5 — Properties of Addition

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Topic A Lesson 5 — Applying Properties to Add Three or More Addends

assessment_source.name: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Sample Items — 3.NBT.A.2 Properties and Multi-Addend Addition

unit_intro_message: 이 레슨에서는 덧셈의 세 가지 성질(교환·결합·항등)을 배우고, 세 수 이상을 더할 때 이 성질들을 활용해 친숙한 합을 만드는 전략을 익힙니다. 어떤 수를 먼저 더하면 가장 빠를지 판단하는 눈을 길러봅시다.
unit_close_message: 잘했어요! 이제 교환·결합·항등 법칙을 사용해 여러 수를 효율적으로 더할 수 있게 되었습니다. 다음 레슨에서는 자리값을 분리(break apart)하는 전략으로 더 큰 수 덧셈을 탐구합니다.
